body_txt: Often seen in Japanese media, this is an attack in which a character jumps really high up into the air and dives foot first from above, usually with the right leg extended forward with the left leg bent at the knee and pointing inward to gain momentum. At risk of stating the obvious , in a realistic fight this would be Awesome, but Impractical . The fighter would probably need to be capable of Video Game Dashing or otherwise maneuvering in midair. But Rule of Cool ensures no one cares, because this kick looks super stylish and dynamic. Popularized in the famous tokusatsu franchise Kamen Rider , and popular enough to be seen as a Signature Move of most, if not all, of the Riders seen throughout that franchise, in which it's called a "Rider Kick". If this trope appears in Japanese media, chances are good that it's an Homage to Kamen Rider (especially if they shout "(insert name here) KIIIIIIIIIICK!!" while doing it). When one kick is not powerful enough, two or more characters may team up to do a Combination Attack version of the move. See also Dynamic Entry and Slide Attack . Compare Goomba Stomp , Ground Pound , Meteor Move . Not to be confused with Divekick , a Fighting Game that plays with this trope.
